project_summary: The project aims to capture the main requirements and constraints for the 0000 - cctns document management system.

functional.text: The system shall allow a user to create a new case.
The system shall allow a user to update an existing case.
For long pages, the system shall provide within-page links to help users jump to the right section.
Each page must always have a clear link leading back to a landmark page they can easily recognize or to the home page.
Users must have the ability to change the menu contents.
Users must be able to resize text.
If a splash screen is used, it must provide a navigation option to skip it.
The system must provide a site map that shows the structure of the site in an overview form.
The navigation must always indicate the user's current location.
If the system is using individualization and/or adaptation for a user, it must be made evident to that user.
The system must provide help directly on the screen.
Help material must be context-sensitive to the user's current task within the application.
Context-sensitive help must be available for all possible actions and scenarios on all user interfaces.
The system must provide an equivalent alternative text-only page to ensure content is always accessible.
Every page or window must provide a clear indication of the user's location in the navigation structure.
The system must support different navigation strategies by offering alternative access paths to the same content.
Users must be able to find support from within the application.
If frames are used for the presentation of help content, an alternative way of presenting the information without frames must be provided.
The system must be able to export audit trails for specified cases for an auditor.
Users must be able to see their user-specified profile on demand.
Users must be able to modify their user-specified profile on demand.
Users must be able to delete their user-specified profile on demand.
Users must be able to track the defects or enhancement requests they have logged.
Users must be able to set up their own persistent defaults for data entry.
Citizens must be able to register their complaints.
Interfaces must be user-configurable to the extent possible.
The system must support a multilingual interface.
The system must be able to display several entities, like cases and suspects, simultaneously.
The system's response when a user tries to access a specific case they do not have rights to must be configurable at configuration time.
A configurable option for when a user tries to access a specific case they do not have rights to is to 'display title and metadata'.
A user must be able to be a member of more than one group.
A user must be able to stipulate which other users or user groups can access their cases.

non_functional.text: Frequently-executed system transactions must be designed to be quick.
Users must be able to complete frequently-executed system transactions with a small number of interactions.
The length of a page must be appropriate for what the user is trying to do.
Within-page links must be clearly distinguishable from links that go to a different page.
Within-page links could have dashed rather than solid underlines to distinguish them.
If a page has a lot of links, the text must still be easy to read.
Color must not be the only means of conveying information.
The system must use ISO 9241-20 as the standard for guidance on ICT accessibility.
All non-text media objects, like graphical images or video, must have equivalent textual descriptions.
The system must avoid using splash screens unless they provide useful content or feedback on the application state.
If frames are used, they must not cause issues with the back button.
If frames are used, they must not cause issues with bookmarking pages.
If frames are used, they must not cause weird scrolling behavior.
User interfaces must allow activation of controls by a variety of input devices, not just a mouse.
Navigation components on the screen must remain consistent with the content being shown.
Help content must be easy to digest, using techniques like short sentences, shorter paragraphs, and bullet points.
Help content must have good spelling and grammar.
The overall design must take into account the general principles of human perception.
Page titles should always be placed in a consistent location on every page.
Empty space filled only with the background color must be used in a way that does not impair the visual skimming of the page.

The system must minimize the number of navigation steps required to get to a piece of content, while considering that different users have different mental models, navigation strategies, and tasks they're trying to complete.
A single set of user interface rules must be used to provide a common look and feel across the entire application.
The user interface must be suitable for users with special needs.
The system should avoid horizontal scrolling wherever possible.
Navigation links must use familiar terminology.
If a user has style sheets or frames turned off, they must still be able to read and understand the page.
The text must always be readable and the spatial arrangement must make sense, even in a simplified view.
User interfaces must follow the guidelines specified under www.usability.gov.
Navigation links must be clearly distinguishable from controls that activate an action.
Links that open new browser windows or pop-up windows must be clearly marked.
The audit trail data stored by the system cannot be modified in any way or deleted by any user.
If the System allows users to make unauthorised attempts to access cases, it must log these in the audit trail.
The system must be able to capture and store security violations.
Error messages must be meaningful to the people who see them.
Error messages must clearly state the reason for the problem.
Error messages must tell the user what actions they can take to resolve the error.
Data validation rules should consult the relevant International Standards for guidance.
Interfaces must be easy to use and intuitive for end users and administrators.
Operational data must not be lost in case of any failure of equipment or the communication network.
The system must be compatible with any specialist software that might be used in the future.
An older case that has not been accessed within the previous 2 months must be displayed on screen in up to 20 seconds.
Case opening times must hold up regardless of the storage capacity or the total number of cases in the system.
Planned maintenance downtime must not exceed <xx> hours in any rolling three month period.
There must be no more than <x> incidents of unplanned downtime in any rolling three-month window.
The total unplanned downtime cannot add up to more than <xx hours/minutes> in any rolling three-month window.
After a major failure, the system must be fully restored, including with inline synchronization, in no more than <xx> hours.
The system must provide role-based control for functionality.
The writing needs to have good spelling and grammar so it's clear and doesn't hurt readability.

data.text: The system must maintain the audit trail for at least the life of the case to which it refers.
Security attributes include access rights, security level, privileges, and password allocation and management.
Persistent default values for data entry must be user-definable.
The system must allow setting up default values for certain fields.
Default values for fields must be user-definable.

business_rules.text: Exporting an audit trail must have no effect on the audit trail stored by the System.
Changes to security attributes for groups or users can be made only by a super-user.
The system must never include a record in a search result list if the user does not have the right to access it.

interfaces.text: Users should be able to resize text using their browser's built-in functions or other appropriate means.
Users must be able to find support through a separate browser interface.
The system must provide an interface for anyone to log any defects or enhancement requests on the application.
